sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: The Basque derby is set to ignite Reale Arena in San Sebastian on 2025-05-04 at 19:00:00 during Round 34 of the La Liga season! Real Sociedad, sitting at 11th with 42 points, host Athletic Club, who are in impressive 4th place with 60 points. This clash promises intensity, given the historical rivalry and the disparity in league standings. The referee, César Soto Grado, oversees the game with an average of 5.2 yellow cards and 0.23 red cards per match, hinting at a potentially feisty encounter. Expect a charged atmosphere as both teams vie for regional bragging rights.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, Athletic Club holds a slight edge with 3 wins compared to Real Sociedad's 2 victories, with no draws recorded. Their most recent encounter on 2024-11-24 saw Athletic Club triumph 1-0 at home, with Oihan Sancet earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.3 Sofascore rating. Head-to-head trends show Athletic Club often strikes first, scoring the opening goal in 5 of their last 7 meetings. Additionally, matches between these teams frequently see more than 4.5 cards (6 out of 8 games) and less than 10.5 corners (8 out of 9 games), suggesting a physical but compact game.

sections.subtitle: Standout Players from Past Encounters
sections.text: In the last 5 matches against Real Sociedad, Athletic Club's Álex Berenguer has been a consistent threat, boasting an average Sofascore rating of 7.24 across 4 starts. Berenguer has netted 2 goals in these fixtures, though he has yet to provide an assist against Sociedad. His presence in midfield could be pivotal again, especially given his knack for influencing games against this opponent. Keep an eye on how he performs alongside teammates like Iñaki Williams in this crucial matchup.

sections.subtitle: Real Sociedad's Key Performers and Stats
sections.text: Real Sociedad will rely on forward Mikel Oyarzabal, who has been in fine form, scoring 5 goals in his last 5 matches with an average Sofascore rating of 7.42. Midfielder Sergio Gómez stands out with 13 big chances created, ranking 5th in the league, alongside 5 assists and 2 goals. Defender Nayef Aguerd impresses with a 72% ground duel win rate, placing him 4th league-wide, while Jon Martin dominates aerially with a 78.8% win rate, also 4th in the league. However, Sociedad struggles offensively, with only 32 goals scored (16th in the league), though they overperform their expected goals by 5.1351, the best in the league. Defensively, they’ve conceded 37 goals (6th fewest) and maintained 12 clean sheets (3rd in the league), but a current streak of 12 games without a clean sheet is a concern.

sections.subtitle: Athletic Club's Key Performers and Stats
sections.text: Athletic Club brings attacking flair with Iñaki Williams, whose 8 assists rank him 3rd in the league, paired with 6 goals and 13 big chances created (5th in the league). Álex Berenguer also contributes significantly, with 5 goals and 7 assists this season, while forward Gorka Guruzeta adds 6 goals to the tally. Unfortunately, they’ll miss key midfielder Oihan Sancet, who has scored 15 goals with a remarkable xG overperformance of 5.6625, due to absence. Athletic Club’s goal distribution shows strength late in games, with 30.77% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes, which could test Sociedad’s endurance.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Team Form
sections.text: Real Sociedad’s strength lies in ball possession, averaging 54.61% (4th in the league), and pressing, with 4.3 possessions won in the final third per game (4th in pressing rank). They also average 5.42 corners per game (3rd in the league), which could be a weapon against Athletic Club. However, their recent form shows no wins in 3 games and a worrying streak of 12 matches without a clean sheet. Athletic Club, on the other hand, has a more balanced attack, with 23.08% of goals scored between the 61st and 75th minutes. Their games often feature under 2.5 goals (7 of 9 recent matches), and they’ve kept corner counts low against Sociedad historically, with less than 10.5 in 4 of their last 5 away games.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Milestones
sections.text: Real Sociedad has converted only 31.75% of their big chances (18th in the league), missing 43 opportunities (11th in the league), which highlights a finishing issue despite creating 63 big chances (11th rank). Their defense, however, excels in limiting opponents’ big chances created, allowing just 55 (2nd fewest in the league). Athletic Club’s late-game scoring prowess is notable, with 12 goals in the last 15 minutes of matches, representing 30.77% of their total. Both teams have physical defenders, with Sociedad’s Jon Martin and Nayef Aguerd ranking high in duels, which could lead to a tightly contested battle in midfield and defense. This matchup also sees Real Sociedad aiming to end a 3-game winless run against a historically tricky opponent.

sections.subtitle: Match Expectations and Challenges
sections.text: Given Real Sociedad’s home advantage at Reale Arena and their pressing ability, they might dominate possession, but Athletic Club’s counter-attacking threat, led by Iñaki Williams, could exploit defensive lapses. Sociedad’s missing players like forward Orri Steinn Óskarsson (3 goals) and defender Igor Zubeldia could hurt their depth. Athletic Club, despite missing Oihan Sancet, still fields a potent lineup with players like Álex Berenguer ready to step up. The referee’s tendency to issue 5.2 yellow cards per game aligns with head-to-head trends of over 4.5 cards in 6 of 8 past meetings, suggesting disciplinary action is likely. With both teams showing streaks of both teams scoring in 6 of Sociedad’s last 8 games, goals at both ends are a possibility.
